varieties.name: Duncan
varieties.traits: Heirloom white grapefruit; Large, round to oblate fruit with smooth pale yellow rind
varieties.flesh_fruit: Buff-colored, very juicy, seedy (30-60 seeds/fruit), superior flavor - considered the standard of excellence

varieties.name: Marsh Seedless (Marsh)
varieties.traits: Heirloom white grapefruit, first seedless variety; Medium to large, round to oblate, smooth pale yellow rind
varieties.flesh_fruit: Buff/pale yellow, very juicy, seedless to few seeds, sweet-tart balanced flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Vigorous, spreading tree, very productive and consistent
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, major Queensland citrus nurseries

varieties.name: Thompson Seedless
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated white grapefruit; Medium-sized, traditional thin yellow-skinned variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale flesh, juicy, traditional grapefruit flavor

varieties.name: Wheeny
varieties.origin: Wheeny Creek, Kurrajong, New South Wales, Australia
varieties.traits: Australian heirloom, pomelo hybrid; Large yellow fruit, medium to thin rind, very fast and strong growing tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale/light-colored, juicy, initially bitter and acidic but becomes sweeter with extended hanging
varieties.yield_notes: Very heavy and reliable fruiting, fruits for extended periods
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Bulleen Art Garden, Australian native citrus specialists

varieties.name: Ruby Red (Redblush)
varieties.traits: Heirloom red grapefruit, first patented citrus variety (1934); Medium-sized, yellow skin with red blush, official state fruit of Texas
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink to light red (3-5x redder than original pink), seedless, distinctly sweeter than white varieties with classic grapefruit tang
varieties.yield_notes: Dense, bushy tree, crops abundantly year after year

varieties.name: Star Ruby
varieties.origin: Texas A&I Citrus Center, 1970 (Dr. Richard Hensz)
varieties.traits: Radiation-bred hybrid from irradiated Hudson grapefruit; Medium-sized, seedless, yellow-orange rind with rose-orange blush, darkest of red varieties
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep dark red/pink, smooth thin peel, tender and juicy, excellent sweet quality with slight tartness
varieties.yield_notes: More difficult to grow than other varieties, lower commercial success

varieties.name: Rio Red
varieties.traits: Radiation-bred hybrid from irradiated Ruby Red budwood (A&I-I-48S); Medium-sized, attractive pink blush on exterior peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red (7-10x redder than Ruby Red), sweeter than standard grapefruit, color retention throughout season
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Queensland citrus nurseries

varieties.name: Flame
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated selection from Henderson; Medium-sized, seedless, yellow peel with eye-catching pink blush, thinner and smoother peel than Rio Red
varieties.flesh_fruit: Intense red color (similar to Star Ruby), evenly distributed, tender and juicy, good sweet-acid balance

varieties.name: Henderson Ruby (Henderson)
varieties.origin: Lower Rio Grande Valley, Texas, 1973 (Samuel Henderson)
varieties.traits: Limb sport mutation from Ruby Red; Seedless, smooth yellow skin with attractive reddish blush, more attractive peel than Ruby Red
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deeper red than Ruby Red (3-5x redder), retains red color longer into season
varieties.availability: Texas citrus suppliers (import possible), specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Ray Ruby
varieties.traits: Limb sport mutation from Ruby Red; Identical to Henderson in appearance, indistinguishable
varieties.flesh_fruit: Even deeper red than Henderson, similar quality

varieties.name: Thompson Pink (Pink Marsh)
varieties.traits: Heirloom pink grapefruit, first pink-fleshed mutation; Large fruit, smooth skin, similar to Marsh parent
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink (shade varies from pale to dark pink based on growing region), few or no seeds, juicy, sweet-tart similar to Marsh

varieties.name: Foster Pink
varieties.traits: Heirloom - first pigmented grapefruit in Florida, limb sport from Walters; Medium-large, very seedy
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow (chamois), turns light pink in right conditions (lighter than other pink varieties)
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity but superseded by Thompson

varieties.name: Oro Blanco (Oroblanco, Sweetie)
varieties.origin: University of California Riverside, 1980 (Drs. Soost & Cameron)
varieties.traits: Triploid hybrid: acidless pomelo × Marsh grapefruit (4N); Round oblate, thick smooth greenish-yellow rind (stays green when ripe), medium size (~9cm diameter)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow, seedless, slightly less juicy than grapefruit, mild and very sweet, lacks bitterness
varieties.yield_notes: Large spreading tree, needs less heat than standard grapefruit, early maturity, holds well on tree
varieties.availability: California Tropical Fruit Tree Nursery (import), specialty citrus suppliers

varieties.name: Melogold
varieties.origin: University of California Riverside, 1986 (Drs. Soost & Cameron)
varieties.traits: Triploid hybrid: acidless pomelo × Marsh grapefruit (4N), sibling to Oro Blanco; Much larger than Oro Blanco (470g vs 360g), more pomelo-like, oblate with slight obconate tendency, smooth to slightly pebbled peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow, seedless, tender, very juicy, mild and sweet reminiscent of pomelo, low acidity
varieties.yield_notes: Vigorous large tree, early maturity (early November-February at Lindcove), holds well on tree

varieties.name: Rio Red
varieties.traits: Radiation-bred hybrid, wide adaptation; Medium-sized, attractive pink blush exterior
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red, very sweet, maintains color throughout season
varieties.availability: Limited in Kerala - try specialty citrus nurseries, import from specialized Indian citrus centers

varieties.name: Star Ruby
varieties.traits: Radiation-bred hybrid; Medium-sized, seedless, darkest red variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep dark red, excellent sweet quality
varieties.yield_notes: More difficult to grow, needs warmth
varieties.availability: Very limited in India, specialty imports only

varieties.name: Flame
varieties.traits: Henderson seedling; Medium-sized, seedless, attractive coloration
varieties.flesh_fruit: Intense red color, tender and juicy

varieties.name: Marsh Seedless
varieties.traits: Heirloom white grapefruit; Medium to large, pale yellow
varieties.flesh_fruit: Buff-colored, very juicy, seedless
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, holds well on tree
varieties.availability: Monsoon Harvest Farms (Abohar, Punjab - organic), ICAR research stations

varieties.name: Red/Pink Pomelo
varieties.traits: Traditional pomelo, indigenous to region; Very large (1-2 kg), thick soft peel (easily peeled), pink to reddish exterior when ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink to red flesh, very juicy, sweet with slight tartness, less bitter than grapefruit
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, 75-100 fruits per tree per year
varieties.availability: Greens of Kerala, Inet Farms Kerala, local Kerala nurseries, widely available

varieties.name: Green/Yellow Pomelo (Babloos)
varieties.traits: Traditional pomelo variety; Very large, thick spongy rind (pale yellow to light green), pear or teardrop shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow to light green, juicy, generally sweeter and less bitter than grapefruit

varieties.name: Devanahalli Pomelo (Chakkota)
varieties.origin: Devanahalli taluk, Bangalore Rural District, Karnataka, India (exclusive GI protection)
varieties.traits: Indigenous Indian heirloom with Geographical Indication status; Large (2-2.5 kg, up to 10 kg possible), twisted trunk tree (10-20cm thick, 5-15m tall)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Unique sweet taste unlike bitter local varieties, superior flavor
varieties.yield_notes: 300-400 fruits per tree annually, flowers twice yearly (Dec/Jan and Jun/Jul), fruit matures in 11 months

varieties.name: Kagzi Pomelo
varieties.traits: Indian pomelo selection; Medium to large size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, characteristic pomelo sweetness
varieties.yield_notes: 50-150 fruits per year depending on conditions

varieties.name: Baneshan Pomelo
varieties.traits: Indian pomelo selection; Traditional variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality

varieties.name: Jabalpur Pomelo
varieties.traits: Regional Indian selection; Named for origin region
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality

varieties.name: Bambai Pomelo
varieties.traits: Indian selection; Regional variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality

varieties.name: Kasoori Pomelo
varieties.traits: Indian selection; Traditional variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality

varieties.name: Assam Red Pomelo
varieties.traits: Indigenous Northeast Indian selection; Red-tinged variety from high rainfall region
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink to red flesh, good quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in high rainfall areas

varieties.name: Coorg Pink Pomelo
varieties.traits: Indigenous South Indian selection; Pink-fleshed variety from Western Ghats region
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink flesh, good sweetness

varieties.name: NRCC Grapefruit-6
varieties.origin: ICAR-Central Citrus Research Institute, Nagpur, India
varieties.traits: Indian research selection on Rough lemon rootstock; Developed for Central Indian conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality adapted to Indian conditions

varieties.name: Imperial (ICAR Selection)
varieties.traits: Indian-adapted variety; Selected for Indian conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality
